political.s: FEC data confirms Burger King's political donations lean right, with $104K raised and 100% of contributions going to Republican-aligned recipients. As a subsidiary of Restaurant Brands International (RBI), the brand operates within a heavily franchised system with limited direct PAC activity. RBI has engaged in some federal lobbying around food, labor, and franchise regulatory matters.

charity.s: The Burger King McLamore Foundation, established in 2005, has awarded nearly $64 million in scholarships to over 54,000 students across North America since 2000. In 2024 alone, the Foundation awarded over $4.2 million in scholarships and $1.1 million in school supplies, plus $1.1 million in emergency relief grants to employees. The Foundation supports education, literacy, and emergency relief across 42 countries.
charity.focus: Education, scholarships, literacy, employee emergency relief

environment.s: RBI, Burger King's parent company, has set science-based targets to reduce GHG emissions by 50% by 2030 versus a 2019 baseline, and achieved a 15% reduction in carbon emissions by 2024. Burger King powers 40% of global restaurants with renewable energy and has committed to 100% sustainable packaging by 2025. However, third-party sustainability scorer DitchCarbon rates Burger King at only 41/100, citing inconsistencies in Scope 2 emissions disclosures and incomplete target-setting at the brand level.

labor.s: Burger King's franchise network has faced serious and repeated labor violations. In 2024, Massachusetts fined a Burger King franchise operator over $2 million for wage theft, minimum wage violations, and child labor law breaches affecting nearly 2,000 workers. In 2026, Wisconsin alleged 1,656 child labor violations at over 100 Burger King locations owned by one franchise operator, Cave Enterprises, ordered to pay $237,000 in back wages and penalties. These recurring violations across multiple states reflect systemic labor compliance failures within the franchise model.

dei.s: Burger King and parent RBI maintain active DEI programs, with Burger King UK earning an inclusion score of 7.9/10 and committing to six formal DEI pledges including executive accountability, bias-free recruitment, and disability inclusion. Glassdoor rates Burger King's DEI at 3.6/5, equal to the restaurant sector average, with improving scores over the past year. Unlike some competitors, Burger King has not publicly rolled back its DEI commitments amid the broader 2025 corporate DEI retreat.

animals.s: Burger King has made significant farm animal welfare commitments, including eliminating battery cages and gestation crates from its U.S. supply chain and committing to 100% cage-free eggs for North and Latin American locations by 2025. RBI also committed to sourcing chicken raised under Global Animal Partnership welfare standards. However, as a major meat-serving fast food chain, Burger King's supply chain inherently involves large-scale animal slaughter, and full implementation of its cage-free pledge has faced delays.

guns.s: Burger King is a restaurant company and does not sell or manufacture firearms. The brand has no disclosed corporate stance or policy specifically on gun sales or gun carry in its locations beyond standard local law compliance.

privacy.s: A California federal judge allowed a class action lawsuit against RBI/Burger King to proceed in 2025, alleging the company placed third-party tracking cookies on users' devices even after they had opted out of data sharing, constituting potential invasion of privacy and fraud. The court found that Burger King's opt-out functionality did not work properly and that users were misled about their data privacy. This active litigation represents a significant data privacy concern for the brand.

execPay.s: RBI CEO Joshua Kobza received total compensation of approximately $14.5 million in the most recently reported fiscal year, while the median Carrols Restaurant Group (Burger King franchise) worker earned approximately $16,987 annually—yielding an estimated CEO-to-worker pay ratio of roughly 1,705:1. RBI's heavily franchised model inflates corporate median pay figures, obscuring the much lower pay of frontline restaurant workers. This pay disparity is among the highest in the quick-service restaurant industry.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
